summary_one_line: ICC header device-class/PCS bytes that drive a multi-output (nOutputChannels != 1) gray-LUT pipeline so case 1 takes the else arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose device-class / colorspace fields produce a single-input, multi-output gray interpolation (nInputChannels==1 but nOutputChannels != 1), so the case 1 arm reaches line 1204 (the else of `nOutputChannels == 1`). Concretely the device-class FOURCC at offset 0x0c is set to a non-mntr class (link/spac) and downstream header bytes (e.g. the DIFFER byte at offset 0x2d = 0x01) configure the LUT to emit more than one output channel.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry device-class FOURCC `link` (6c 69 6e 6b) or `spac` (73 70 61 63) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and 0x01 at offset 0x2d, which routes the profile to a 1-input N-output gray LUT; per-role coverage shows W exclusively hits lines 1204/1206/1209 (the else arm taking the false side) and W invokes Eval1Input 8 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry device-class FOURCC `mntr` (6d 6e 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and 0x00 at offset 0x2d, which yields a 1-input 1-output gray LUT; in the per-role overlay L hits the true side (LinLerp1D) and never enters the else arm — the false branch of line 1196 stays blocked (L1196 T=110 F=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP. The header FOURCC / configuration bytes are compared against fixed expected literals during ICC parsing; cmplog logs those comparands and I2SRandReplace splices the correct bytes directly into the input at the matching offsets, flipping the profile into the multi-output configuration that takes the false side. value_profile only gets a coarse Hamming-distance CMP_MAP bucket and cannot place the exact multi-byte token, so it never resolves the branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the false side of `nOutputChannels == 1` inside the case 1 (single input channel) arm.
Taking the winning side means entering the else arm at lines 1204-1209, hit only by W.
Winner-resolving seeds carry a non-mntr device-class FOURCC (link/spac) at the header offset that loser seeds set to mntr.
Loser-blocking seeds carry device-class FOURCC mntr at the same header offset.
Offset 0x2d is the single byte the diff flags as fully DIFFER between W (0x01/0x90) and L (0x00).
The winner takes the false side of L1196 while the loser never does (L1196 T=110 F=0 for loser).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1196  T=312 F=40  T=110 F=0  if (nOutputChannels == 1) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The else arm leads to Eval1Input, invoked 8 times by W and 0 times by L.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator while value_profile does not.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, leaving only CMP_MAP gradient feedback.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2129 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 1d8091807e46b328` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 12 of the 13-level ancestor chain, the characteristic ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the false side on an exact multi-byte header token shows value_profile resolving it as often as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), which would mean the divergence is not driven by exact-literal substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact program-side meaning of the DIFFER byte at offset 0x2d (whether it directly sets nOutputChannels>1) is inferred from header layout, not confirmed against lcms parsing source in the prompt.
